source.context: Bogotá 'Cartel de la Cardiología'. A councilman (concejal) reviewing public-health contracting detected a pattern: a single provider winning most hemodinamia/cateterismo contracts at sub-reference prices, providers logging impossibly short inter-procedure times, and an avalanche of PQRs mentioning WhatsApp/Telegram payments and 'facturas falsas'.

_procedures: The cartel scenario implies *implausible monthly volume*. The agent's monthly cap for cardiologia_intervencionista is 120; we keep 30 in `clinical.procedures` here for compactness and augment the medical-agent batch via the data_loader with extra records (see _cartel_helpers.make_data_loader).

pqrs.text: Me pidieron pagar la cuota por WhatsApp y me dieron una factura que parece falsa.
Llegan auxiliares a captar pacientes al hospital y los remiten a la misma IPS por Telegram.
Una señora en la sala me dijo que la comisiona la dan por whatsapp al médico.

_comment: SECOP-shaped rows that mirror https://www.datos.gov.co/resource/rpmr-utcd.json. Generated for offline testing of the Contracting Agent's SECOP-fed reference-price check.
